mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive mean or Rank IC greater than 0.02 during rate hike periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, it signals labor market slack and impending corporate earnings deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate profit margins, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases during early-cycle periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate economic activity, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple quarterly release cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 255.0, it signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the inflation threshold loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of monthly observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which immediately pressures retail sector earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative retail sales changes fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60 percent of instances during late-cycle periods, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in out-of-sample tests.
